title: Provide a way to use the cli to list the file paths that would be in a build context
body:
### Feature request description

Currently, the best known (accurate) way to list the files in a build cntext is to run a build and evaluate the context from inside the container build (e.g. `COPY` everything and then `ls -lR` it or something similar). This is slow (either relatively speaking compared to what's possible, or in practice if the context is very large) and a bit onerous to set up without impacting the actually desired container build (requires a secondary Containerfile or similar).

It would be very helpful to have a way to have the podman cli output a list of the file paths that would be in the build context for a given build. I am aware of two use cases for this, both of which I have encountered recently:

- Confirming that a .containerignore file functions as designed. There are some subtleties to .containerignore files and being able to test them without actually spinning up a container and copying the build context would be nice.
- Serving as a Makefile prerequisite generator (similar to `gcc -M`). Being able to output the exact list of file paths in the build context would make it much easier to construct Makefiles that will rebuild a container if (and only if) anything in the build context changes.

### Suggest potential solution

Possible solutions include:
- adding a flag to podman build that doesn't actually build but only outputs the context file paths, maybe `--only-output-context`
- adding a new command to the cli, maybe `podman context list`

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I am currently attempting to use custom logic in bash script with `find` and `awk` to try and achieve the same thing, but that is unproven and unstable (if the .containerignore syntax ever changes, it will stop working correctly).

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: @Salynn I have a question, the person who is building the image will always have access to `build context` right ?  Why can't they just do `ls` ?

@flouthoc `ls` doesn't take into account ignorefile rules or files included in the context from outside the directory (such as out-of-directory Containerfiles). Also, `ls` doesn't (on its own) take into account the `--build-context` option. It would be much more convenient to be able to have podman output the list of files in the build context, rather than recreate a bunch of logic, keep track of bug fixes and changes in that logic, and make changes to build context lists in two or more places.

This would be great, I always lacked this in the docker world, but later stumbled upon https://github.com/johnstairs/dockerignore-filter, which is IMHO exactly the thing, but I never made myself use it as it's a 3rd party project which I'm hesitant to adopt for questionable availability of support (not feeling like supporting it myself).

My use case is somewhat similar to the second one in the OP, but my goal is to have a commit-bound CI build skip a build on changes outside of the build context.

Hello,
Can I work on this in case this feature request is valid?
